name: Puig de la Creu and Sant Sadurní de Gallifa Loop
name_original: Sant Sadurní de Gallifa y Puig de la Creu - circular gravel desde Caldes de Montbui
description: This difficult 29.1-mile circular gravel cycling route from Caldes de Montbui offers panoramic views and challenging terrain.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Caldes De Montbui navigate a landscape characterized by rolling hills and river valleys. The region features varied terrain, from the gentle gradients found along river paths to more challenging ascents leading to higher elevations. This area provides a mix of natural environments, including open countryside and sections that follow watercourses, suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Caldes De Montbui The most popular touring cycling route is Puig de la Creu and Sant Sadurní de Gallifa Loop, a 29.1 miles (46.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation changes, offering challenging climbs and expansive views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Besòs Riverside Park – Besòs River Bike Path loop from Caldes de Montbui, a moderate 37.0 miles (59.6 km) path. This trail follows the Besòs River, offering a more gentle ride through riverside landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Font de la Carrerada – Riera de Caldes loop from Caldes de Montbui, a 21.3 miles (34.3 km) trail leading through riverine areas, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Touring cycling around Caldes De Montbui is defined by rolling hills, river valleys, and varied elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy touring bike trails to more advanced routes. The routes in Caldes De Montbui are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Caldes De Montbui's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Caldes De Montbui?
A: There are nearly 30 touring cycling routes around Caldes De Montbui, offering a diverse range of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Caldes De Montbui?
A: Yes, Caldes De Montbui offers several easy touring cycling routes. For a more gentle ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33797797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de la Carrerada – Riera de Caldes loop from Caldes de Montbui&lt;/a&gt;, which follows riverine areas and has minimal elevation gain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Caldes De Montbui?
A: The region is characterized by rolling hills and river valleys, offering varied terrain. You'll find everything from gentle gradients along river paths to more challenging ascents leading to higher elevations, providing a mix of open countryside and watercourse-following sections.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Caldes De Montbui are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31548540" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Besòs Riverside Park – Besòs River Bike Path loop from Caldes de Montbui&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate circular ride along the river.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e971553040" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de la Creu and Sant Sadurní de Gallifa Loop&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation changes and demanding climbs. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39552140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont de Castellar del Vallès – Sant Llorenç Savall loop from Caldes de Montbui&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What attractions or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The area around Caldes De Montbui is rich in natural and historical sights. You can explore stunning waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/236272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel del Fai Monastery and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1478633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of Sot del Bac&lt;/a&gt;. There are also several castles, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/649491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sentmenat Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/643524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montbui Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which can be found near various routes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Caldes De Montbui?
A: The touring cycling routes in Caldes De Montbui are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from gentle river paths to challenging climbs, and the beautiful natural environments that offer a mix of open countryside and watercourses.

Q: Are there any routes that offer good viewpoints?
A: Many routes that involve ascents into higher elevations will reward you with expansive views of the surrounding landscape. For example, routes that climb towards areas like Puig de la Creu or Coll del Farell offer panoramic vistas.

Q: Is there public transport access to the trailheads?
A: While Caldes De Montbui is accessible by public transport, specific connections to individual trailheads can vary. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes connecting to the start points of your chosen cycling tour.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by cafes or places to stop for refreshments?
A: Many routes in the region pass through or near small towns and villages where you can find cafes and restaurants. Planning your route with komoot allows you to discover points of interest, including refreshment stops, along the way.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Caldes De Montbui?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in Caldes De Montbui, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be hot, especially in the midday sun, while winters are cooler but often still suitable for cycling on clear days.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families with children?
A: For families, look for routes with gentler gradients and shorter distances, often found along the river valleys. These paths provide a safer and more enjoyable experience for younger riders, away from heavy traffic.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Caldes De Montbui?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Caldes De Montbui, particularly near the town center or designated recreational areas. For specific routes, it's best to check the route details on komoot, as many tours provide information on nearby parking options.
tips: Mountain of 668 meters on horseback from the municipalities of Castellar del Vallès and Sentmenat, in the region of Vallès Occidental. At the top there is a castle built between the 16th and 17th centuries and is listed as a Cultural Property of National Interest.

Sant Llorenç Savall is a municipality in the region of Vallés Occidental, province of Barcelona, Catalonia, Spain.

   It is located in the northern part of its region, on the border with those of Bages el Moyanés and Vallés Oriental. Among its historical-artistic heritage, the ruins of the castle of La Pera and the Romanesque church of Sant Feliu de Vallcarca stand out, as well as the remains of a prehistoric dolmen.

Sant Sadurní de Gallifa or de la Roca is a Romanesque hermitage in the municipality of Gallifa, in Vallès Occidental. It is at the top of La Mola, at an altitude of 942.3 meters. It is northeast of the town of Gallifa, at the southern end of the Serrat Punxegut. It gives its name to the Cingles de Sant Sadurní, which surround almost all of La Mola, Turó de la Pinassa and Morral del Puig. It is on the grounds of the La Roca farmhouse, which is why it is also known as Sant Sadurní de la Roca. It is a building that forms part of the Inventory of the Architectural Heritage of Catalonia.

There is evidence of the location since 939 when Ató sold to the Levite Guadamir a farm that shared the domus (Vulgar Latin term to refer to a house or building) of Sant Sadurní. In a bull of Pope Benedict VII from the year 978 in which the limits of the Bishopric of Vic are defined, the mountain of Sant Sadurní is shown. In a document from the year 1063, it is recorded that a certain Bonfill gave the church two mancusos (a medieval gold coin weighing four grams). From the Mangush-engraving Arabic, this allows us to assume that important works must have been done there.

In 1316 it was a priory of the Portella monastery and, while it depended on it, there was a small community and, always, at least one priest. In the 17th century the building was in a very bad state and the bishop forbade, in 1604, mass to be celebrated there.

(Source: https://ca.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sant_Sadurn%C3%AD_de_Gallifa)

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this gravel cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular gravel route is considered difficult for touring cycling. It demands a good level of physical fitness and technical skill due to its challenging terrain, including steep ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain, primarily consisting of gravel tracks and paths ('senderos' and 'pistas'). You'll encounter steep ascents and descents, and some sections may be technical or involve 'almost lost paths' ('caminos casi perdidos'), requiring attention to orientation. Wet conditions can make the ground particularly challenging.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable features and scenic views. You'll pass by the historic Puig de la Creu Castle and Viewpoint, the charming settlement of Sant Llorenç Savall, and the ancient Hermitage of Sant Sadurní de Gallifa (10th Century). The trail also provides panoramic views, especially during ascents and from Sant Sadurní de Gallifa.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular gravel route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area, offering a continuous journey without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this gravel cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Caldes de Montbui. While specific parking locations aren't detailed, you should look for suitable public parking within Caldes de Montbui to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Thanks to the forested areas providing shade, this route is recommendable even in summer. However, be mindful that steep sections can be particularly challenging if the ground is wet, so dry conditions are generally preferable for gravel cycling.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: This route is located within the Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac. Generally, there are no entrance fees for accessing the natural park itself. However, always check local regulations for specific activities or protected areas within the park, though permits for cycling are typically not required on established trails.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As the route passes through the Parc Natural de Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac, dogs are generally allowed but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Sender de Sant Llorenç Savall, de Sant Nicolau a Sant Llorenç Savall, Matagalls-Montserrat, and Vallès Natural. Variant de Sant Llorenç Savall, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this difficult gravel cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring a well-maintained gravel or touring bicycle, appropriate safety gear including a helmet, sufficient water and snacks, a repair kit, and navigation tools (like a GPX device or the komoot app) as some paths might require attention to orientation. Layered clothing is also recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for this route?
faqs.answer: The ascent to Collet de Sant Sadurní is known for being quite technical, with existing tire marks suggesting it's popular with 'trialeros'. Be prepared for steep sections and potentially loose gravel. While forested areas offer shade, always check weather conditions, as wet ground significantly increases the challenge on descents. This route is a great way to explore more remote areas away from asphalt roads.
